title: bidens bigelovii var. angustiloba
intro: Bidens bigelovii var. angustiloba, also known as narrow-leaved beggar's lice, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The narrow-leaved beggar's lice plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Herbal preparations of Bidens bigelovii var. angustiloba include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
